# SRC009 - Measuring the Effects of Advertising: The Digital Frontier

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Measurement |
| Authors / source | Lewis, Rao & Reiley |
| Year | 2013/2015 |
| Source type | Academic chapter / working paper |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w19520/w19520.pdf](https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w19520/w19520.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Researchers connected to platforms; still rigorous and cautionary.

## Core takeaway

Digital metrics create opportunity for causal measurement but also false inference; clicks are not the whole effect.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to require incrementality language in measurement plans.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC009
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
